title: parosela weberbaueri
intro: Parosela weberbaueri, also known as the Peruvian tree daisy, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial agent. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Parosela weberbaueri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically prepared by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
